video:video.video:title: Meryl proud to anchor major movie at 76
video:video.video:description: Meryl Streep is happy to anchor a major movie like "The Devil Wears Prada 2" at nearly 77.

video:video.video:title: Rwanda, Africa's most densely populated nation, tries to protect its farmland
video:video.video:description: Since September, Rwanda's government has been mapping agricultural land and using satellite imagery to track any development encroaching on farmlands and forests in a country where the population is expected to reach 22 million in a couple of years.

video:video.video:title: How worms are helping to create healthy soil for Kenyan farmers
video:video.video:description: Worms are helping improve soil on Kenyan farms. A venture just outside the capital uses the creatures to eat agricultural waste and animal dung, turning it into nutrient-rich compost.

video:video.video:title: Digital detox: welcome to the offline club for those wanting a screen-free connection
video:video.video:description: A new kind of club has emerged in Amsterdam - one where participants choose to go offline, away from their phones and other devices. (AP video by Aleksandar Furtula)

video:video.video:title: How biometrics are revolutionising EU travel
video:video.video:description: A new digital border system set to replace passport stamping across much of Europe will be fully implemented from 10 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr on seeing Beatles bandmate Paul McCartney in concert
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr reflects on watching Paul McCartney perform an intimate show at the Los Angeles Fonda Theatre in March.

video:video.video:title: Streep was 'miserable' on first 'Devil Wears Prada'
video:video.video:description: As she launches sequel "‘The Devil Wears Prada 2" in Seoul, acting royalty Meryl Streep explains why she was "kind of miserable" when filming the first movie in the fashionable franchise.

video:video.video:title: Republican Clay Fuller wins election to U.S. House in Georgia’s 14th District
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller on Tuesday won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia, turning back a Democratic challenge with the help of President Donald Trump’s endorsement despite uneasiness over the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts release images of moon flyby and eclipse
video:video.video:description: A total solar eclipse greeted the three Americans and one Canadian as the moon temporarily blocked the sun from their perspective.

video:video.video:title: Sweeney's love for 'Euphoria' character
video:video.video:description: Sydney Sweeney reveals how Cassie - her character in hit drama "Euphoria" - has changed her life.

video:video.video:title: Meryl 'miserable' on first 'Devil Wears Prada'
video:video.video:description: Meryl Streep says filming "‘The Devil Wears Prada" was "miserable" as she launches the sequel in Seoul.

video:video.video:title: Preserving rural Jewish life is the focus of a Maine center this Passover
video:video.video:description: The Center for Small Town Jewish Life at Colby College is growing as Passover ends this year. The center began a decade ago with a goal of supporting Jewish congregations that are far from big cities.

video:video.video:title: Iran, the US and Israel agree to a temporary ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel reached a tentative, two-week ceasefire Wednesday in the war that tore across the Middle East and disrupted the global energy market, with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya attends 'Euphoria' premiere in LA
video:video.video:description: Zendaya strutted down the carpet on Hollywood Boulevard to celebrate "Euphoria's" season three premiere in Los Angeles, Tuesday. (April 7)

video:video.video:title: 'Euphoria' cast reunites at LA premiere
video:video.video:description: Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney stun at the launch of the new season of 'Euphoria' in Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia extradites Scottish man wanted by Spain as alleged crime boss
video:video.video:description: Steven Lyons, described by law enforcement as a senior figure in an international crime syndicate, was flown from Bali to Amsterdam early Wednesday morning, according to a spokesperson for the island's immigration office.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at Rio de Janeiro Olympic Park velodrome
video:video.video:description: Dozens of firefighters were battling a large blaze at Rio De Janeiro's Olympic Park Velodrome on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: 'Malcolm in the Middle' cast returns for more chaos
video:video.video:description: Bryan Cranston, Frankie Muniz, and Jane Kaczmarek reunited Tuesday in New York to celebrate the new Hulu limited series “Malcolm in the Middle: Life’s Still Unfair."

video:video.video:title: Baby camel born in UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Sophia was born to Bactrian camels mum Izzy and dad Oakley at Whipsnade Zoo in Bedfordshire, boosting the herd’s total to eight.

video:video.video:title: Vance calls US-Iran ceasefire a 'fragile truce'
video:video.video:description: Speaking at an event in Hungary, U.S. Vice President JD Vance called the ceasefire agreement “a fragile truce” but offered no details.

video:video.video:title: Oil drilling in Brazil's Amazon draws concern from Indigenous communities
video:video.video:description: Last year, Brazil's Petrobras began exploratory oil drilling off the Amazon coast, drawing concern from environmental groups and Indigenous communities.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 8th: Fire and smoke In Beirut after Israeli airstrike; Freed French detainees from Iran arrive in Paris; Artemis II crew calls ISS crew members; Harlem Globetrotters meet Pope Leo XIV

video:video.video:title: James McAvoy comes clean about lying for an audition
video:video.video:description: James McAvoy makes his directorial debut with "California Schemin'" - a true story about a rap duo from Dundee who pretended to be American while seeking success - something that McAvoy can relate to after trying on various accents to secure film roles.

video:video.video:title: Stocks rally worldwide following a ceasefire with Iran, oil plunges toward $90
video:video.video:description: Stock markets are surging worldwide, and oil prices are plunging back toward $90 per barrel after President Donald Trump pulled back from his threat to force a “whole civilization” to die in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Harlem Globetrotters teach Pope Leo to spin basketball on his finger
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV welcomed the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter’s Square on Wednesday. He was seen attempting one of the team’s classic tricks — spinning their red, white, and blue basketball on a finger — with help from one of the players

video:video.video:title: Huge fire burns in central Beirut after consecutive Israeli airstrikes hit the city
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes hit several dense commercial and residential areas in central Beirut without warning Wednesday, hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Israelis and Iranians voice their thoughts on Iran ceasefire news
video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel agreed to a two-week ceasefire, an 11th-hour deal that headed off U.S.

video:video.video:title: James Marsden shakes things up in 'Your Friends & Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: James Marsden plays new character Owen Ashe - a millionaire with an extravagant lifestyle and many secrets - who disrupts the wealthy community of Westmont Village in season 2 of Apple TV's "Your Friends & Neighbors."

video:video.video:title: Streep 'happy to represent' women over 70
video:video.video:description: As she launches sequel "‘The Devil Wears Prada 2" in Seoul, acting royalty Meryl Streep discusses a recent photoshoot with fellow 76-year-olds Annie Leibovitz and Anna Wintour.

video:video.video:title: Daniil Medvedev smashes racket seven times in 6-0, 6-0 defeat
video:video.video:description: Russia's Daniil Medvedev had a racket smashing moment in his 6-0, 6-0 defeat at Monte-Carlo Masters on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Strikes hit Beirut as Israel vows to continue attacking Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israel's attacks came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the US-Israeli war with Iran. Israel has said the agreement does not extend to Lebanon, although mediator Pakistan said it does, and vowed to continue its ground invasion in the south.

video:video.video:title: Attorneys, more family arrive to court hearing for Gilgo Beach serial killier
video:video.video:description: Rex Heuermann accused in a string of long-unsolved slayings known as the Gilgo Beach killings is expected to plead guilty, closing a case that bedeviled investigators, agonized victims’ relatives and tantalized a true-crime obsessed public for years.

video:video.video:title: Barrage of strikes seen in Beirut after Israel vows to continue attacking Lebanon despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A barrage of strikes was seen across Beirut on Wednesday after Israel vowed to continue attacking Lebanon despite a ceasefire with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Finland's plan to bury spent nuclear waste not without risk to future generations
video:video.video:description: After decades of construction, the world’s first facility for permanently disposing spent nuclear fuel is set to begin operations in Finland, becoming a final resting place for tons of dangerous radioactive waste.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon left rocked by Israeli barrage as search for those missing in Beirut rubble continues
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes hit busy commercial and residential areas in central Beirut without warning on Wednesday, hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: BTS kicks off world tour in front of excited fans
video:video.video:description: A huge crowd of BTS fans packed into a rainy Goyang Stadium near Seoul to see the K-pop megastars kick off world tour, "ARIRANG."

video:video.video:title: BTS launches world tour in South Korea
video:video.video:description: A huge crowd of BTS fans packed into the Goyang Stadium near Seoul on Thursday to see the K-pop supergroup kick off their long-awaited world tour, "ARIRANG." (April 9)

video:video.video:title: New species of spider captured on video for the first time
video:video.video:description: A new species of spider has been captured on video for the first time. Researchers in southern Spain are racing to learn more about the tiny Cryptodrassus michaeli arachnid and the ecosystem it inhabits.

video:video.video:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claims victory over Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is claiming victory over Iran, saying all parties agreed to a two-week ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices plunge and the Dow surges in a worldwide rally following tentative ceasefire with Iran
video:video.video:description: Oil prices are plunging back toward $95 per barrel, and stock markets are surging worldwide after President Donald Trump pulled back from his threat to force a whole civilization to die in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Wife, daughter of accused Gilgo Beach killer arrive as he prepares to plead guilty
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect accused in a string of long-unsolved slayings known as the Gilgo Beach killings is expected to plead guilty, closing a case that bedeviled investigators, agonized victims’ relatives and tantalized a true-crime obsessed public.

video:video.video:title: Wife of Gilgo Beach murder suspect urges focus on victims, asks for privacy
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect has pleaded guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

video:video.video:title: 'A sign of true hope': Pope Leo XIV hails two-week ceasefire between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has welcomed the announced ceasefire in the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran as a “sign of true hope” and called for diplomacy to prevail in that and other conflicts.

video:video.video:title: Oil drilling in Brazil's Amazon draws concern from Indigenous communities
video:video.video:description: Last year, Brazil's Petrobras began exploratory oil drilling off the Amazon coast, drawing concern from environmental groups and Indigenous communities.

video:video.video:title: March smashes record as hottest in continental US in 132 years
video:video.video:description: March’s persistent unseasonable heat was so intense that the continental United States registered its most abnormally hot month in 132 years of records, according to federal weather data.

video:video.video:title: American gas prices remain high amid ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has strained global fuel supplies, as the Strait of Hormuz remains a vital choke point for international trade.

video:video.video:title: Small victories for Democrats won't guarantee a midterm success, according to a political science expert
video:video.video:description: Dr. Zachary Peskowitz, a political science professor at Emory University, urges caution when interpreting results.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee librarian fired for not relocating LGBTQ books
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee library board has fired the county’s top librarian for refusing to comply with its vote to move more than 100 LGBTQ books from the children’s to the adult section over its claims that they promote “gender confusion.”

video:video.video:title: Mexican miner rescued after nearly 14 days in flooded Sinaloa mine
video:video.video:description: A Mexican miner, who had been trapped for nearly 14 days following a collapse in the northern state of Sinaloa, was rescued alive on Wednesday, authorities said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Petroleum analyst explains Iran ceasefire deal's impact on US fuel costs
video:video.video:description: Patrick De Haan of GasBuddy cautions that a lot of details about the ceasefire have yet to be worked out and any pace of declines in gasoline prices will vary from state to state and even city to city.

video:video.video:title: 15-year sentence for 'Ketamine Queen' in Matthew Perry case draws swift reactions
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has handed down a sentence of 15 years in prison to a woman who pleaded guilty to selling “Friends” star Matthew Perry the ketamine that killed him in a 2023 overdose.

video:video.video:title: Attorney, fiancee seek answers after ICE shoots, detains man in California
video:video.video:description: An attorney for a man shot by federal ICE agents during an arrest in central California says his client didn't try to run over officers with his car and disputed claims he has a warrant out for his arrest in El Salvador.

video:video.video:title: Long Island architect Rex Heuermann admits he killed eight women in the Gilgo Beach case
video:video.video:description: Rex Heuermann entered the pleas on Wednesday which brought finality to a case that bedeviled investigators, tantalized the public and spawned true-crime documentaries.

video:video.video:title: Beirut residents react as Israeli strikes impact neighborhoods
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes hit several commercial and residential areas in central Beirut without warning on Wednesday, hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutor praises victims’ families as relatives express relief after Heuermann guilty plea
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleaded guilty on Wednesday to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu says Israel still has more objectives to complete in Iran despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Wednesday that there are "more objectives to complete" in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Oil plunges below $95 as the Dow surges 1,100 in a worldwide rally following a ceasefire with Iran
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 leaped 2.5% after Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, less than 90 minutes before a deadline Trump had set for it to open the Strait of Hormuz and allow oil tankers to exit the Persian Gulf.

video:video.video:title: Long Island architect Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to murdering 7 women
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleads guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 8th: US, Israel and Iran agree to tentative ceasefire; Israeli strikes central Beirut, killing scores ; Rex Heuermann pleads guilty in the Gilgo Beach killings; Charles and Camilla celebrate 21st wedding anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Al Jazeera journalist killed by Israeli drone strike in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Israel's military killed Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah on Wednesday in a drone strike that targeted his car in the Gaza Strip, the media network and Palestinian health officials said.

video:video.video:title: London basks in hot sunshine - for now
video:video.video:description: London basks in hot sunshine - for now

video:video.video:title: Ocean protections clash with mining pressure in Indonesia's most diverse marine ecosystem
video:video.video:description: Raja Ampat, a remote archipelago in eastern Indonesia, has been long regarded as a model for ocean conservation.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Pooh Shiesty's lawyers comment following court hearing
video:video.video:description: A federal judge on Wednesday said rapper Pooh Shiesty will remain in custody until trial after he and eight others were accused of kidnapping three music industry professionals and robbing them at gunpoint in Texas.

video:video.video:title: Shopkeepers worried about impact of 'smart lockdown' over fuel price rises
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s federal government has ordered markets and shopping malls to close in the country by 8:00 p.m., as a fuel crisis deepens in the region.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter
video:video.video:description: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning balances drama and comedy in latest role
video:video.video:description: Fanning stars in an Apple TV adaptation of Rufi Thorpe's novel, "Margo's Got Money Troubles." (April 8)

video:video.video:title: Utility bills are exceeding mortgages in West Virginia despite Trump’s promised cuts
video:video.video:description: President Trump promised to cut electricity bills, but prices have risen instead. Many in West Virginia now face utility costs surpassing rents and mortgages.

video:video.video:title: Finland's plan to bury spent nuclear waste not without risk to future generations
video:video.video:description: After decades of construction, the world's first facility for permanently disposing spent nuclear fuel is set to begin operations in Finland, becoming a final resting place for tons of dangerous radioactive waste.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 9
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, April 9th: Mourners mark 40th day after death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Palestinians bid farewell to Al Jazeera journalist who was killed in a drone strike; China affirms commitment to secure global supply chain; Octopus fossil determined to not be an octopus after a

video:video.video:title: Giant snails and tiny insects threaten a way of life on rice and crawfish farms
video:video.video:description: Farmers in Louisiana are battling pests ranging from large apple snails that clog crawfish traps to tiny insects called delphacids.

video:video.video:title: Lack of Hollywood movies in Cannes due to 'moment of transition'
video:video.video:description: Thierry Frémaux - the artistic director of the Cannes Film Festival - has said not to expect red carpet premieres like “Top Gun: Maverick” or “Mission: Impossible — The Final Reckoning” - both of which made splashy premieres in recent years - at the 2026 edition.

video:video.video:title: Iran marks 40-day mourning ceremony for slain supreme leader
video:video.video:description: In the capital Tehran, mourners wearing black began their rally from Jomhouri Eslami Square to the neighborhood of the office of Ali Khamenei in the heart of Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Ocean protections clash with mining pressure in Indonesia
video:video.video:description: Nickel mining, tourism and plastic pollution are putting pressure on Raja Ampat, one of the most biodiverse marine ecosystems on earth.

video:video.video:title: Argentina approves Milei's bill that eases protections for glaciers, despite environmental backlash
video:video.video:description: Argentina's Congress has approved a controversial bill easing glacier protections to boost mining investments.

video:video.video:title: Taraji P. Henson says biggest adjustment for Broadway is 'one day off'
video:video.video:description: Taraji P. Henson says the toughest part of Broadway is having just one day off, but admits it keeps her sharp, alert, and ready, reawakening her theater muscle every day.

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context
video:video.video:description: Elle Fanning had to explain Only Fans to Nicole Kidman for new show context

video:video.video:title: Traffic through Strait of Hormuz as Iran closes key waterway
video:video.video:description: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks on Lebanon, Iranian state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Even after a ceasefire agreement, the fate of Iran's missile and nuclear program is unclear
video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel agreed Tuesday to a two-week ceasefire, an eleventh-hour deal that headed off U.S.

video:video.video:title: Armenian Orthodox church in Tehran commemorates Iranian supreme leader’s death
video:video.video:description: An Armenian Orthodox church commemorated on Wednesday the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. (AP video by Bassam Hatoum)

video:video.video:title: Price hikes hit Greeks shopping in preparation for Orthodox Easter celebration
video:video.video:description: Price increases due to the US-Israel war on Iran have hit Greek shoppers as they prepare to celebrate Orthodox Easter on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Search and rescue efforts to find survivors after Israeli strikes kill at least 182 in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: The attacks in Lebanon came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew saw things they 'had never even imagined'
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II mission crew held a news conference from on board the Orion spacecraft on Wednesday, as Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen said he saw things he "had never even imagined."

video:video.video:title: Harlem Globetrotters team members teach Pope Leo to spin basketball on finger
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV enjoyed a lighthearted moment Wednesday as he welcomed the U.S. exhibition basketball team the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter’s Square.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon rocked by deadliest wave of Israeli attacks since start of conflict
video:video.video:description: Lebanon rocked by deadliest wave of Israeli attacks since start of conflict

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong and other Asian markets retreat on concerns of fragile Middle East ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Oil rose again to above $97 a barrel and Asian stocks were trading lower Thursday on skepticism over a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

video:video.video:title: Apple’s iPod is making a comeback
video:video.video:description: Secondhand sales of Apple iPods are surging four years after it was killed off, with young people showing a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by algorithms.

video:video.video:title: New Yorkers flock to Manhattan park for lovable woodcocks' bobbing strut
video:video.video:description: American woodcocks came to New York City looking to strut their stuff, and New Yorkers fell in love. (AP video: Emily Wang Fujiyama, Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: One Tech Tip: A new generation is reviving the iPod for distraction-free listening
video:video.video:description: Remember the iPod? It's making a quiet comeback. Four years after Apple killed off its digital music player, secondhand sales are surging, It's fueled in part by young people interested not just in its retro looks but a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by

video:video.video:title: Sophia the Bactarian camel born at UK zoo
video:video.video:description: There was a fluffy new addition to the UK's largest zoo as it prepares to celebrate its 200th birthday.

video:video.video:title: NATO chief says allies are not ‘whistling past the graveyard’
video:video.video:description: Secretary-General Mark Rutte dismisses NATO's 'early obituaries' and says allies are not ‘whistling past the graveyard’ a day after meeting U.S.

video:video.video:title: Why is Israel striking Lebanon?
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes on Wednesday without warning killed at least 203 people and wounded more than 1,000, Lebanon’s health ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Route 66 is paved with 100 years of hardship and hope
video:video.video:description: One of the world's most famous highways marks its centennial this year. Known for its neon lights and roadside diners, Route 66 boasts a rich and complicated history that has lured motorists for generations.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate as BTS return to the stage
video:video.video:description: Fans react to BTS’ return to the stage after nearly four years, at the opening night of the K-pop megastars' "ARIRANG" world tour in Goyang, South Korea. (April 9)

video:video.video:title: Police block protesters marching toward Venezuela presidential palace
video:video.video:description: A group of protesters attempting to march to Venezuela's Presidential Palace in Caracas were stopped by police on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to opening night of BTS' world tour
video:video.video:description: Fans react to BTS’ return to the stage after nearly four years, at the opening night of the K-pop megastars' "ARIRANG" world tour.

video:video.video:title: Police block protesters marching toward Venezuela’s presidential palace
video:video.video:description: The demonstrators were trying to reach the building in Caracas to demand action on the country’s political prisoners.

video:video.video:title: Miner found alive in flooded Mexico mine after being trapped for two weeks
video:video.video:description: Rescue teams pulled a trapped miner to safety after nearly two weeks inside a flooded gold mine in northwestern Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Newly created Polymarket accounts win big on well-timed Iran ceasefire bets
video:video.video:description: A group of new accounts on the prediction market Polymarket made highly specific, well-timed bets on whether the U.S. and Iran would reach a ceasefire on April 7, resulting in hundreds of thousands of dollars in profits for these new customers.

video:video.video:title: Debbie Allen praises sister Phylicia Rashad as inspiration
video:video.video:description: Debbie Allen speaks about her sister Phylicia Rashad at a press event for "Joe Turner’s Come and Gone," praising Rashad’s influence on her career and her work in theater.

video:video.video:title: Family of missing Navajo woman still waiting for answers
video:video.video:description: On June 15, 2021, Ella Mae Begay's truck was seen leaving her Sweetwater, Arizona home before dawn. Five years later, the missing woman's family is still seeking answers.

video:video.video:title: 'I am not Epstein's victim': First lady Melania Trump says lies 'need to end today'
video:video.video:description: In a rare statement from the White House, first lady Melania Trump denied any ties to Jeffrey Epstein and knowledge of his crimes, saying the "images and stories are completely false" and urging Congress to hold a public hearing for Epstein's victims.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 9
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, April 9th: Approved Israel-Lebanon talks to take place in Washington; Deadliest Israeli strikes in Lebanon; Melania Trump denies Epstein ties in White House statement; Russian spy submarines deterred in North Atlantic.

video:video.video:title: Gene Simmons on his son's musical collab with Paul Stanley's son
video:video.video:description: Here's what Gene Simmons thinks about his son's musical collaboration with KISS member Paul Stanley's son.

video:video.video:title: Dave Chappelle celebrates with residents at opening of new building for Ohio radio station
video:video.video:description: The comedian stood on the front lawn of a newly restored 19th-century schoolhouse Thursday, joining neighbors and local officials as a small-town radio station secured its future in the community he calls home.

video:video.video:title: 'I thought I was dead': Survivor of Israeli strike on Beirut recalls from hospital
video:video.video:description: A day after intense Israeli strikes pounded Lebanon’s capital, survivors on Thursday recounted scenes of carnage as hospitals struggled to cope with a surge of casualties.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelan police block protesters demanding higher wages and pensions
video:video.video:description: Union leaders, retirees and public sector workers in the Venezuelan capital marched Thursday toward the presidential palace to demand higher wages and dignified pensions, only to be met by police blockades.

video:video.video:title: Peruvians choosing a president from 35-candidate pool in Sunday's election
video:video.video:description: International observers arrived in Lima ahead of the country's presidential election on Sunday. Voting is mandatory for Peruvians from the ages of 18 to 70.

video:video.video:title: White House defends Trump's 'moral high ground' after threat to Iran's 'whole civilization'
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt defended President Donald Trump’s warning that a "whole civilization will die," insisting he retains the "moral high ground" against Iran and calling the remark a "very strong threat" that delivered results.

video:video.video:title: Iranian American recalls fear after Trump threat to destroy Iran’s civilization
video:video.video:description: Hossein Baninajar, an Iranian American who has lived in the U.S. for 11 years, said he feared losing his mother and sister if President Donald Trump had followed through on a threat to destroy Iran’s civilization Tuesday night.

video:video.video:title: Polymarket bets on Iran ceasefire surged before Trump's announcement
video:video.video:description: A group of new accounts on the prediction market Polymarket made highly specific, well-timed bets on whether the U.S. and Iran would reach a ceasefire on April 7, resulting in hundreds of thousands of dollars in profits for these new customers.

video:video.video:title: What Marshawn Lynch's former teammates said about his ‘Euphoria’ debut
video:video.video:description: Former NFL star Marshawn Lynch is one of the newest “Euphoria” cast members — and his teammates had questions. Season 3 of the HBO Max series premieres on April 12.

video:video.video:title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons visits Detroit Rock City to open new eatery
video:video.video:description: Gene Simmons visited Detroit Rock City to celebrate the grand opening of a restaurant co-founded by him and his Kiss bandmate Paul Stanley (April 9 - AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Melania Trump denies ties to Epstein
video:video.video:description: First lady Melania Trump is denying ties to Jeffrey Epstein and knowledge of his sex crimes, saying Thursday that the “stories are completely false” and calling accusations that she was somehow involved “smears about me.”

video:video.video:title: James McAvoy comes clean about lying for an audition
video:video.video:description: James McAvoy shares what happened when he lied for an audition. His directorial debut "California Schemin'" will premiere in theaters in the U.K. on April 10.

video:video.video:title: Air travelers are feeling the pinch as jet fuel costs climb
video:video.video:description: A new reality is setting in for air travelers worldwide: rising fees, fewer flight options and difficult decisions about whether a trip is worth the cost.

video:video.video:title: An unofficial ban keeps BTS at bay in China
video:video.video:description: BTS has launched their first world tour in over four years, but China is notably absent from the tour dates An unofficial ban has barred K-pop from the Chinese market, prompting entertainment companies to pivot toward Japan and North America.

video:video.video:title: Israel's main airport resumes full operations as Iran ceasefire holds despite friction
video:video.video:description: Israel’s main airport resumed full operations on Thursday after the Iran war stranded tens of thousands of people, including both Israelis abroad and visiting tourists.

video:video.video:title: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi lands in Pyongyang for two-day North Korea visit
video:video.video:description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi arrived in North Korea on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Science to ancient octopus fossil: you’re not an octopus after all
video:video.video:description: Newly published research by the University of Reading concludes that fossilized remains listed by Guinness World Records as the earliest known octopus belong instead to a relative of a nautilus, a cephalopod with both tentacles and a shell.

video:video.video:title: British PM Starmer visits UAE on Middle East tour to reinforce 'fragile' ceasefire
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer arrived in the UAE during a visit to allied countries in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 3-9 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Measles threatens babies in South Carolina and the nation as community vaccination rates decline
video:video.video:description: Babies too young to be vaccinated are among the most vulnerable in measles outbreaks like the one in South Carolina, which is the largest the U.S. has seen in decades.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians mourn Al Jazeera journalist Mohammad Wishah targeted by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians gathered Thursday morning at Al-Aqsa hospital in central Gaza to bid a final farewell to Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah, who was killed in an Israeli drone strike the day before.

video:video.video:title: Patriarch Theophilos III washes feet of Orthodox priests in Jerusalem at ceremony ahead of Easter
video:video.video:description: Patriarch Theophilos III washed the feet of Orthodox priests in Jerusalem on Thursday in a ceremony made more special by the reopening of the Holy Sepulchre.

video:video.video:title: Melania Trump denies ties to Epstein and Bondi won't appear for House deposition into the investigation
video:video.video:description: First lady Melania Trump denied ties to Jeffrey Epstein and knowledge of his sex crimes. The announcement Thursday comes after the Department of Justice indicated that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not appear for a scheduled deposition next week.

video:video.video:title: Citizen 'Frog Patrol' helps amphibians survive dangerous mating season road crossing in Poland
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of volunteers have joined a "Frog Patrol" initiative in a forest near the Polish capital of Warsaw to help amphibians survive road crossings during their spring mating rituals.

video:video.video:title: Sardi’s, the iconic Broadway restaurant, changing hands in June, caricatures will stay on the wall
video:video.video:description: For nearly a century, Sardi’s has been a staple of the New York City theater district. Now, the current owner is ready to retire and past the torch, along with the caricatures, to an organization that made it all possible so many years ago.

video:video.video:title: Britain celebrates late Queen Elizabeth II as a style icon to mark her centenary
video:video.video:description: Britain celebrates late Queen Elizabeth II as a style icon to mark her centenary

video:video.video:title: Bodies of two cats removed from rubble of Beirut buildings destroyed by Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Rescuers from a Lebanese animal welfare NGO recovered the bodies of two cats from the rubble of buildings destroyed by the Israeli aerial assault on Beirut the previous day.

video:video.video:title: Chaos and panic during strikes captured in Beirut girl's viral Snapchat video
video:video.video:description: Naya Fakih was walking with her father on Corniche Mazraa Street on Wednesday afternoon, recording a Snapchat video.
